corpus.title: US and Iran trade strikes as Tehran blocks tankers from Strait of Hormuz
corpus.signal_text:
US and Iran trade strikes as Tehran blocks tankers from Strait of Hormuz

The US has been striking Iran for 12 consecutive nights, hitting targets across the nation. Tehran has warned of a powerful retaliation in the event of attacks on civilian infrastructure. Iran also announced that it stopped three oil tankers trying to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

corpus.title: US attacks Iran for 13th consecutive night
corpus.signal_text:
US attacks Iran for 13th consecutive night

The US military says it has completed its 13th night of strikes against Iran.

corpus.title: Trump vows to hit an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship Iran attacks
corpus.signal_text:
Trump vows to hit an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship Iran attacks

President Trump threatened Wednesday to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant, including in Tehran, each time Iran attacks a ship in the Strait of Hormuz. Why it matters: Trump sent the new threat as he considers both a ceasefire and a significant military escalation. His key condition for a ceasefire is for Iran to stop its attacks on ships and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.Driving the news: U.S. Central Command said Iran has attacked more than 30 commercial vessels over the past three months.

corpus.title: US to destroy bridges, plants in retaliation to Iranian attacks: Trump
corpus.signal_text:
US to destroy bridges, plants in retaliation to Iranian attacks: Trump

U.S. President Donald Trump warned Wednesday that Washington would destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant each time Tehran attacks a ship transiting the strategic Strait of Hormu...

corpus.title: US launches new strikes on Iran over shipping routes threat
corpus.signal_text:
US launches new strikes on Iran over shipping routes threat

The US said the latest strikes were aimed at weakening the threat Iran poses to commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The US has now attacked Iran for 13 consecutive nights. DW has more.

corpus.title: “Not a single drop of oil”: Iran issues stark warning to US over infrastructure threats
corpus.signal_text:
The Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters stated in a press release on Wednesday, that US President Donald Trump has resumed threatening Iran with strikes on its infrastructure.
“The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, and if any vessel is permitted passage, it will proceed solely along the designated route and under the previously announced protocols,” the headquarters’ leadership said in the statement.
The statement added that if the United States carries out its threats, the Iranian Armed Forces will not allow “even a single drop of oil to be exported,” warning that oil, gas, electricity, and economic infrastructure throughout the region will become targets for attacks.
It further stressed that repeated threats from the US and its military will only broaden the scope of the war across the region, and potentially beyond.
Trump had previously declared that “if Iran fires a missile, shell, drone, or any other weapon at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz at any time, the United States will bomb and destroy a bridge or power station—including those located in or near the capital, Tehran.

corpus.title: US strikes cut roads and bridges in southern Iran as ground assault talk grows
corpus.signal_text:
US strikes cut roads and bridges in southern Iran as ground assault talk grows

US strikes have destroyed bridges, tunnels and rail links across southern Iran in a seventh straight night of attacks, severing roads to the Strait of Hormuz coast and feeding talk in Washington and Tehran that a ground assault may follow.

corpus.title: Why US strikes are moving deeper into southern Iran
corpus.signal_text:
Why US strikes are moving deeper into southern Iran

The latest US strikes on southern Iran may offer the clearest indication yet of how Washington's campaign around the Strait of Hormuz is evolving from retaliation to the systematic degradation of Iran's military infrastructure.

corpus.title: US military says 13th consecutive night of attacks on Iran completed
corpus.signal_text:
US military says 13th consecutive night of attacks on Iran completed

US military says 13th consecutive night of attacks on Iran completed The US Central Command (Centcom) said it "completed" its latest wave of strikes against Iran for the 13th night in a row. The attacks lasted more than two hours, with Centcom saying it targeted military command centers, drone storage facilities, communication networks, coastal surveillance sites and maritime capabilities.

corpus.title: Iran-US War Live: US Strikes Iran Again, Bahrain Warns Of Incoming Fire As War Escalates
corpus.signal_text:
Iran-US War Live: US Strikes Iran Again, Bahrain Warns Of Incoming Fire As War Escalates

US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump had threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for every attack on shipping in Hormuz, drawing an "eye for an eye" warning from Tehran as both sides sharpened threats in the escalating war.

corpus.title: US Strikes Iran As Tensions Escalate Over Shipping Routes In Hormuz
corpus.signal_text:
US Strikes Iran As Tensions Escalate Over Shipping Routes In Hormuz

The US military announced Thursday that it is conducting the 13th night of strikes against Iran as clashes escalate over shipping routes.

corpus.title: 'Attack A Ship In Strait Of Hormuz, Lose A Bridge Or Power Plant': Trump Issues Direct Warning To Iran
corpus.signal_text:
'Attack A Ship In Strait Of Hormuz, Lose A Bridge Or Power Plant': Trump Issues Direct Warning To Iran

U.S. President Donald Trump issued a stern warning to Tehran, stating that the United States will target Iranian critical infrastructure, including power plants and bridges in Tehran, in response to any future attacks on maritime vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

corpus.title: US Enters 12th Night Of Strikes On Iran; Jordan, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia On Alert As Strait Of Hormuz Crisis Escalates
corpus.signal_text:
US Enters 12th Night Of Strikes On Iran; Jordan, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia On Alert As Strait Of Hormuz Crisis Escalates

The US carried out a 12th night of strikes on Iran over the Strait of Hormuz as Trump threatened to bomb a bridge or power plant for each attack, while oil prices surged and Iran vowed an "eye for an eye" response amid stalled diplomacy.

corpus.title: US hits Iran as Gulf states targeted in flareup over Hormuz
corpus.signal_text:
US hits Iran as Gulf states targeted in flareup over Hormuz

The United States struck Iran Monday for a second day running, drawing Tehran's reprisals against US allies in the Gulf as the foes battle over the status of the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

corpus.title: US strikes Iran for third day, will reimpose blockade
corpus.signal_text:
US strikes Iran for third day, will reimpose blockade

The US launched a third night of strikes on Iran as President Trump planned to reimpose a blockade Tuesday on Iranian ports and floated a 20 percent fee on cargo in the vital Strait of Hormuz.

corpus.title: US military says it completed 13th consecutive night of strikes on Iran
corpus.signal_text:
US military says it completed 13th consecutive night of strikes on Iran

WASHINGTON, July 23 - The U.S. military said late on Thursday it completed its latest round of strikes on Iran that marked a 13th consecutive night of American attacks.
